heading: Delete a Single Document (deleteOne())
body:
You can delete a single document from a collection using the
[collection.deleteOne()](collection.deleteOne) method.

The following [Atlas Function](atlas-functions) snippet deletes one document
in the `items` collection that has a `name` value of `lego`:

```javascript
const query = { "name": "lego" };

itemsCollection.deleteOne(query)
  .then(result => console.log(`Deleted ${result.deletedCount} item.`))
  .catch(err => console.error(`Delete failed with error: ${err}`))
```

Alternatively, you can update a single document using
[collection.findOneAndDelete()](collection.findOneAndDelete). This method allows you to
find, remove, and return the deleted document in a single operation.

codeBlocks.content:
const query = { "name": "lego" };

itemsCollection.deleteOne(query)
  .then(result => console.log(`Deleted ${result.deletedCount} item.`))
  .catch(err => console.error(`Delete failed with error: ${err}`))
